rationale: Medicine L4 takes each L3 fellowship and decomposes into the named clinical entities, procedural categories, or drug classes practitioners self-identify with. Cardiology fellowships split by patient population + procedure type + device class. Oncology tumor-type leaves split by molecular driver + treatment modality. Surgery L3 leaves split by anatomical region or named procedure. I went 6-12 L4 per L3 where the field warrants it; some smaller L3s get 4-6.

uncovered_concerns: v2 update: 152 → 248 L4 leaves. Filled in the L2 specialties whose L3 children previously had no L4 (anesthesiology, pain, emergency, radiology subs, theranostics, pathology subs, lab medicine, PMR, sports medicine, medical genetics, addiction, occupational, dentistry, OMF surg, plastic surg, transplant surg, pediatric surg, GP, FM) plus added remaining gaps in surgical specialties.
Total L4 medicine: 248 leaves across the 110 L3 leaves (~2.3 L4 per L3 average; ranging from 1 leaf at small L3s to 6-7 at oncology, cardiology, surgery).
